questions.question_text: Why can't we reconcile $12M in daily transaction volume across three systems?
questions.answer_summary: The payment gateway, ledger, and settlement system each use different cutoff times (UTC, EST, and PST respectively). During the 3-hour overlap window, transactions are double-counted in one system and missing from another, creating a persistent $12M daily variance.

questions.question_text: Are we settling with merchants accurately or are we leaking basis points?
questions.answer_summary: Interchange fee tiers are mapped using a static table from Q2 2024. Card network rate changes in October shifted 18% of transactions to higher tiers. We've absorbed $340K in unrecovered interchange over 5 months.

questions.question_text: Is our fraud rate really below the network threshold or is it a measurement gap?
questions.answer_summary: The fraud dashboard measures confirmed fraud as a share of approved transactions. It excludes chargebacks still in dispute and doesn't count friendly fraud. Including these raises the effective rate from 0.08% to 0.14%, above Visa's monitoring threshold.

questions.question_text: How confident are we in the AML reports we're filing with FinCEN?
questions.answer_summary: The transaction monitoring system flags based on rolling 30-day aggregates, but the aggregation resets at month boundaries instead of rolling forward daily. Structuring patterns that span month-end are systematically missed.

questions.question_text: Why does our payment volume growth look flat when new merchant sign-ups are up 30%?
questions.answer_summary: New merchant onboarding counts activated accounts, but 42% of new merchants haven't processed a single transaction after 60 days. Volume growth from active merchants is 8%, masked by the denominator including dormant accounts.
